name: Pros & cons
when_to_use: 需要把一个方案的支持理由与反对理由并排审视时用它，适合选型结论、政策取舍、架构决策和购买建议；若要比较三个以上方案及多项统一指标，应改用 comparison-table，若需要打分权重则用 rubric-grid。
why_valuable: AI 的利弊清单常失去视觉平衡、把解释和结论混在一起；本模板固定绿色 Pros／红色 Cons 双栏、数量提示与可选净结论，让读者一眼看清取舍，并在窄屏自动变为纵向。

data_in.note: data 是一个决策主题及两组要点。pros 与 cons 按原顺序渲染，建议各 2–6 项；每项可用对象给出主句 text 和补充 detail，也兼容纯字符串。verdict 可省略；tone 只接受 'positive'、'negative'、'mixed'，未知值按 mixed 的琥珀色处理。

schema.title?: string —— 标题；省略时显示 “Pros & cons”
schema.summary?: string —— 置于标题下的决策背景或范围，建议一句话
schema.pros: {text:string,detail?:string}[] —— 支持理由；text 为主句，detail 为可选解释；也兼容 string[]
schema.cons: {text:string,detail?:string}[] —— 反对理由；字段语义同 pros
schema.verdict?: {label?:string,text:string,tone?:'positive'|'negative'|'mixed'} —— 可选净结论；label 默认 “Net verdict”，tone 默认 mixed

spec.note: 按要点数量自动增高；桌面端两栏，620px 以下单栏。无 vh/百分比高度，不测量载体尺寸。

en.when_to_use: Use it to examine supporting and opposing reasons for one option side by side, including technology selections, policy tradeoffs, architecture decisions, and purchase advice. For three or more options with shared metrics, use comparison-table; when weighted scoring is needed, use rubric-grid.
en.why_valuable: AI-generated pros/cons lists often lose visual balance and mix explanation with conclusion. This template fixes green Pros and red Cons columns, count cues, and an optional net verdict so tradeoffs are clear at a glance, then stacks vertically on narrow screens.

data_in.note: data contains one decision topic and two sets of points. pros and cons render in source order; 2-6 items each is recommended. Each item may be an object with a main text sentence and supplemental detail, and plain strings are also accepted. verdict is optional. tone accepts only 'positive', 'negative', or 'mixed'; unknown values use mixed amber styling.

schema.title?: string -- title; displays "Pros & cons" when omitted
schema.summary?: string -- decision context or scope below the title; one sentence is recommended
schema.pros: {text:string,detail?:string}[] -- supporting reasons; text is the main sentence and detail is an optional explanation; string[] is also accepted
schema.cons: {text:string,detail?:string}[] -- opposing reasons; fields have the same semantics as pros
schema.verdict?: {label?:string,text:string,tone?:'positive'|'negative'|'mixed'} -- optional net conclusion; label defaults to "Net verdict" and tone defaults to mixed

spec.note: Height grows with the number of points. Layout uses two columns on desktop and one below 620px. No vh or percentage heights, and carrier dimensions are not measured.

example._why: 用一个真实的架构决策展示对称的 3×3 要点与 mixed 净结论，能同时验证绿/红语义、详情行和琥珀色 verdict。

minimal.title: Should we adopt a monorepo?
minimal.summary: Decision brief for a six-person product team maintaining web, API, and shared UI packages.

pros.detail: Update the API contract and every consumer in one reviewed change.
Shared linting, TypeScript, and release conventions stay aligned.
Engineers can trace dependencies without switching repositories.

cons.detail: Affected-project detection and caching become important as the repo grows.
Repository permissions are less granular than separate repos.
History, pipelines, and ownership rules need a deliberate transition.

verdict.label: Worth it—with guardrails
verdict.text: Adopt the monorepo for shared product code, but require affected-only CI, CODEOWNERS, and independent package releases.

uplink: 纯 display 组件，无 uplink；所有状态均由 data 决定，不向 AI 或宿主回传选择。

carriers.artifact: supported:boot.artifact 已在 sandboxed iframe 中以 example.minimal 原样渲染验证；CSS 与 DOM 全部位于自有 shadow root
carriers.widget: supported:与 artifact 同一零依赖模板，仅 parent 改为 shadowRoot；可在载体 shadow 内再挂自有 shadow
carriers.codex: supported:与已验证的 artifact boot 相同，document.getElementById('viz') 为唯一载体挂载点

isolation.evidence: self-authored inline-template, owns its shadow, zero global footprint; rendered-verified
